title: Platform — Anybuild
description: The Anybuild platform: one model for data, views, workflows, UI, reporting, and security—built to define, run, and change systems over time.

hero.headline: Model-driven platform for business systems
hero.copy: Anybuild starts from a shared model and keeps data, workflows, views, UI, reporting, and security aligned. AI speeds up parts of the work while the platform keeps structure, rules, and lifecycle.

model.title: Model
model.text: Entities, relations, constraints, rules, default values.

row1.title: Views
row1.text: Reusable data layer for UI, reporting, and logic.

row1.title: Workflows
row1.text: Reusable and nestable processes with triggers and actions.

row2.title: UI
row2.text: Generated screens plus custom components when needed.

row2.title: Security
row2.text: Built-in access control extended into the domain.

run.title: Run and change
run.text: Systems evolve on top of the same structure instead of being rebuilt.

highlight.title: One model
highlight.leadHtml:
Data, views, UI, workflows, reporting, and security use the same structure.
Change it once and it updates everywhere.
highlight.note: Hosting, database, domains, and security are already included. No extra setup.

paths.title: Access
paths.text: Security applies across all parts, not per screen.

howBuilt.label: How systems are defined
howBuilt.title: More than one way to build
howBuilt.copy: Start from text, diagram, point-and-click, or code. All of them work on top of the same platform.

strip.title: AI
strip.text: Generate the first version from requirements or a spec.

strip.title: Diagram
strip.text: Define entities and relations visually through ERD and graph views.

strip.title: Manual and code
strip.text: Use designers, SQL, and custom UI when more control is needed.

components.label: Core components
components.title: System layer
components.copy: All parts work together. Not separate tools.

items.title: Data model
items.summary: Entities, relations, schemas, constraints, business rules, default values.
details: The model is the foundation of the system.
It defines structure, validation, relationships, and behavior in one place.
Everything else - UI, workflows, views, reporting - builds on top of it.

items.title: Views & SQL
items.summary: Reusable data layer with design mode and SQL code mode.
details: Define data using visual tools or SQL.
Views are reused across UI, reporting, workflows, and integrations.
Encourages modular logic and avoids duplication.

items.title: Workflows
items.summary: Reusable, nestable processes for business logic.
details: Combine smaller workflows into larger processes.
Used for automation, state changes, and system behavior.

items.title: Triggers
details: Run workflows when something happens in the system or externally.
Supports real-time reactions and background processing.

items.title: Security
items.summary: Built-in roles, users, and extendable domain security.
details: Apply permissions not just globally but within business structures.
Security is enforced consistently across the system.

items.title: UI & Widgets
details: Forms and layouts are generated from the model.
Custom widgets and full pages can be created and extended.
Supports internal and external UI development.

items.title: Reporting & Spreadsheets
details: Use views as data sources for reports.
Spreadsheet engine supports complex layouts like financial reports.
Multi-user, structured alternative to Excel.

items.title: Functions & Logic
details: Define logic once and reuse across the system.
Supports both design mode and SQL-based implementation.
Encourages modular, maintainable systems.

items.title: Field types
items.summary: Structured data definitions reused across entities.
details: Define reusable field types with validation and behavior.
Ensures consistency across the data model.

items.title: APIs & Integrations
items.summary: Custom routes, webhooks, and external APIs.
details: Define custom API routes and handle them with workflows.
Workflows can call other workflows, use views, read and write data, and apply full system logic.
Connect to any external API. If an API exists, Anybuild can use it.
Prebuilt connectors (for example Microsoft Office) are available, but there is no limitation.

lifecycle.label: Lifecycle
lifecycle.title: Built to change
lifecycle.copy: Systems do not stop after the first version. The same platform is used to define, run, and change them over time.

strip.title: Define
strip.text: Create structure through AI, diagrams, designers, or code.

strip.title: Run
strip.text: Use the platform foundation for security, workflows, views, UI, and reporting.

strip.title: Change
strip.text: Update the model and keep extending the same system instead of rebuilding it.

highlight.title: Already included
highlight.leadHtml: Authentication, roles, permissions, triggers, scheduling, reusable views, autogenerated UI, reporting, hosting, database, and domains are already part of the platform.

platformAi.label: AI
platformAi.title: AI where it helps
platformAi.copy: Use AI across requirements, modeling, SQL, UI, and application features. It accelerates work while the platform keeps structure, rules, and lifecycle.

sectionCta.copyHtml: Build on a structured system layer with built-in model, workflows, views, reporting, UI, and security. Use AI to speed up parts of the work, not to replace the platform.
